pattern: Since the chosen subarrays must be distinct, the answer is the sum of the k largest values of max-minus-min taken over all subarrays.
hints: Each subarray may be used at most once now, so you want the k subarrays with the biggest spread.
Every subarray has a value of max minus min; you simply need the k largest of those values and their sum.
Compute, for every starting index, the running max and min as the right end extends, collect all subarray values, and sum the k largest.

edge_inputs.description: three distinct subarrays sharing the full spread
single element, only one subarray of value 0
all equal values, every subarray value is 0
k equals the total number of subarrays

stress.description: random array with k up to the number of subarrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 10**9 + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    total = n * (n + 1) // 2
    k = rng.randrange(1, min(100000, total) + 1)
    return [nums, k]

stress.description: narrow value range so many ties appear among subarray values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 6) for _ in range(n)]
    total = n * (n + 1) // 2
    k = rng.randrange(1, min(100000, total) + 1)
    return [nums, k]
